card.coreExplanation: because 后面必须接完整从句（主语+谓语），用来直接说明原因；常放在主句前或后，前置时多用逗号隔开，后置时紧跟主句。
card.usageNote: 语气中性偏直接，口语和写作都常用，适合解释迟到、拒绝、决定、结果等；不适合需要委婉或只想“顺带一提”原因的场景（可用 since/as 更弱）。because 从句不能单独成句；前置 because 从句后通常加逗号，后置时一般不加。初学者先用“主句 + because + 从句”最稳。

slotSchema.label: Main clause (result/decision)
slotSchema.hint: 这里放结果/决定/状态的完整句子。常错：只写短语或漏主语。例：I stayed home.

slotSchema.label: because + clause (reason)
slotSchema.hint: because 后必须是完整从句(主语+动词)。常错：because of + 名词误用到这里。例：because it was raining.

slotSchema.label: Comma when because-clause is first
slotSchema.hint: because 从句在前时，后面通常用逗号隔开；在后时一般不用。常错：两边都乱加逗号。例：Because I was tired, I left early.

commonErrors: I stayed home because of it was raining.｜because of 后不能接从句｜改：I stayed home because it was raining.
Because I was tired.｜because 从句不能单独成句（除非回答why的省略语境）｜改：I left early because I was tired.
Because I was tired I left early.｜前置 because 从句后通常要逗号，读起来更清楚｜改：Because I was tired, I left early.
I stayed home, because it was raining.｜主句+because 从句通常不在 because 前加逗号（除非强停顿）｜改：I stayed home because it was raining.
Because of I was sick, I didn’t go.｜because of 后应接名词/动名词，不接从句｜改：Because I was sick, I didn’t go. / Because of my illness, I didn’t go.
I didn’t go because I was sick, so I stayed home.｜重复因果连接，句子累赘｜改：I didn’t go because I was sick. / I was sick, so I stayed home.

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构：because + 从句；because of + 名词/动名词。语气：两者都直接，但 because of 更像“原因短语”，更紧凑。对比：I stayed home because it rained. / I stayed home because of the rain.

contrasts.title: since + clause (reason)
contrasts.note: 结构：since + 从句；because + 从句。语气：since 更像“既然/鉴于”，更委婉、常用于对方已知原因；because 更强调直接因果。对比：Since you’re busy, we can meet tomorrow. / We can’t meet because you’re busy.

contrasts.title: as + clause (reason)
contrasts.note: 结构相同都接从句，但 as 表原因更弱、更书面，常用于背景说明；because 更明确回答“为什么”。对比：As it was late, we left. / We left because it was late.

contrasts.title: due to + noun
contrasts.note: 结构：due to + 名词短语（常接 be 动词后或更正式句式）；because + 从句更口语自然。语气：due to 更正式/公文。对比：The delay was due to traffic. / The train was late because there was heavy traffic.

contrasts.title: so + result (result connector)
contrasts.note: 结构：because 引出原因；so 引出结果。语气：so 更像叙述结果链条。对比：I stayed home because it rained. / It rained, so I stayed home.

examples.sentence: I stayed home because it was raining.
examples.translation: 因为在下雨，我待在家里。
examples.note: 最基础：主句 + because + 从句。

examples.sentence: Because I was tired, I went to bed early.
examples.translation: 因为我累了，所以我早早上床睡觉了。
examples.note: 前置 because 从句，后面用逗号。

examples.sentence: The meeting was canceled because the manager was ill.
examples.translation: 会议取消了，因为经理生病了。
examples.note: 较书面但仍常用。

examples.sentence: I didn’t go because I was sick, not because I was lazy.
examples.translation: 我没去是因为我生病了，不是因为我懒。
examples.note: 对比敏感：用 not because 修正原因。

examples.sentence: I’m calling because I have a question about my bill.
examples.translation: 我打电话是因为我对账单有个问题。
examples.note: 电话/客服常见表达。

examples.sentence: She smiled because she recognized him.
examples.translation: 她笑了，因为她认出他了。
examples.note: 叙事场景里交代原因。

templates.label: Safest beginner
slotHints: MAIN：结果/决定（完整句）
REASON：原因（主语+动词的完整从句）
templates.exampleSentence: I can’t come, because I have to work.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我不能来，因为我得工作。

templates.label: Alternate position (fronted reason)
slotHints: REASON 放前面后加逗号
MAIN 放后面陈述结果
templates.exampleSentence: Because I missed the bus, I was late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 因为我错过了公交车，所以我迟到了。

templates.label: Writing-oriented (neutral formal)
slotHints: RESULT 用更客观的结果表达（was canceled/was delayed 等）
REASON 用清晰事实原因从句
templates.exampleSentence: The flight was delayed because the weather was bad.
templates.exampleTranslation: 航班延误了，因为天气不好。

templates.label: Correction/contrast reason
slotHints: REASON A 是真实原因
REASON B 是你要否定的误会原因
templates.exampleSentence: I left because I felt sick, not because I was bored.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我离开是因为我不舒服，不是因为我觉得无聊。

card.coreExplanation: “since + 从句”用来说明原因，语气常表示“既然/由于（这是已知背景）”，引出主句的结论或决定。结构要求since后接完整句子（主语+谓语），常放句首并用逗号隔开，也可放句中。
card.usageNote: 语体偏中性到正式，写作、邮件、说明理由时自然，尤其当原因是对方已知道或你把它当作共同前提。若要强调强烈因果或回答“为什么”，更常用because。口语聊天可用但别频繁；注意since放句首后通常加逗号，放句中一般不加逗号。初学者先用句首模板：Since + 完整原因句, + 结果/决定。

slotSchema.label: since 后的原因从句
slotSchema.hint: 放“主语+谓语”的完整原因句。常错：只放名词/短语。例：Since it’s raining, we’ll stay home.（不是*Since the rain）

slotSchema.label: 主句（结论/决定）
slotSchema.hint: 放结果、决定、建议等。常错：缺主语或用不完整句。例：Since you’re busy, I’ll call later.

slotSchema.label: 逗号（句首常用）
slotSchema.hint: 句首since从句后常用逗号分开两句；句中一般不加。例：Since we’re late, let’s take a taxi.

commonErrors: *Since the bad weather, we canceled the trip. —— since后必须接从句，不是名词短语；改：Since the weather was bad, we canceled the trip. / Because of the bad weather, ...
*Since he was sick. He stayed home. —— since从句不能单独成句；改：Since he was sick, he stayed home.
*Since you are tired you should rest. —— 句首since从句后通常要逗号，阅读更清楚；改：Since you are tired, you should rest.
*I left early, since I was tired, so I went home. —— 重复因果连接词导致结构累赘；改：I left early since I was tired. / Since I was tired, I left early.
*Since do you need help, call me. —— since不是疑问词；改：If you need help, call me. / When you need help, call me.
*Since I live here for 10 years, I know the area. —— since表原因时从句时态要自然；这里应是现在完成/完成时态；改：Since I have lived here for 10 years, I know the area well.

contrasts.title: because + clause
contrasts.note: 结构：两者都接从句；because更直接回答“为什么”，因果更强。语气：because更口语、更强调；since更像“既然/鉴于”。对比：Because I’m tired, I’m leaving.（强调原因）/ Since you’re here, let’s start.（把原因当共同前提）

contrasts.title: as + clause (reason)
contrasts.note: 结构：都可句首+逗号；as更轻、更书面，理由更弱，常用于叙述流程。语气：as较正式、客观；since稍强、带“既然”。对比：As it was raining, we stayed in.（平铺叙述）/ Since it was raining, we’ll stay in.（基于该前提做决定）

contrasts.title: because of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构：because of后接名词/动名词；since后必须接从句。场景：想用短语原因选because of；想说明完整事实选since。对比：Because of the rain, we stayed home. / Since it was raining, we stayed home.

contrasts.title: due to + noun
contrasts.note: 结构：due to后接名词短语，常搭配be动词或更正式结构；since接从句。语气：due to更正式、报告体；since较通用。对比：The delay was due to traffic. / Since there was traffic, the train was delayed.

contrasts.title: since (time) vs since (reason)
contrasts.note: 结构：时间since常配完成时，表示“自从”；原因since不表示时间，只引出理由。场景：时间谈经历用since time；做决定解释用since reason。对比：I have lived here since 2020.（时间）/ Since you’re free, let’s meet.（原因）

examples.sentence: Since it’s raining, we’ll stay home.
examples.translation: 由于在下雨，我们就待在家。
examples.note: 最安全的句首模板：原因作背景+决定。

examples.sentence: I’ll send the report now since you need it today.
examples.translation: 既然你今天需要，我现在就把报告发过去。
examples.note: 句中since：通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: Since the results are preliminary, further analysis is required.
examples.translation: 由于结果仍是初步的，仍需要进一步分析。
examples.note: 偏书面/正式表达。

examples.sentence: Because I forgot my wallet, I can’t pay. (Not: Since I forgot… if it’s new info)
examples.translation: 因为我忘带钱包了，所以我付不了钱。（若是新信息，用because更自然）
examples.note: 对比敏感：原因是否为对方已知/共同前提。

examples.sentence: Since you’ve finished your homework, you can play games.
examples.translation: 既然你已经写完作业了，你就可以玩游戏。
examples.note: 常用于“既然…就…”的许可/建议。

examples.sentence: Since we’re short on time, let’s take a taxi.
examples.translation: 由于时间不够，我们坐出租车吧。
examples.note: 常见于会议/出行的快速决策。

templates.label: Safest beginner template (sentence-initial)
slotHints: {reason_clause}=完整句：主语+谓语（事实/背景）
{decision/result_clause}=决定/结果/建议（完整句）
templates.exampleSentence: Since I’m busy today, I’ll reply tomorrow.
templates.exampleTranslation: 由于我今天很忙，我明天再回复。

templates.label: Alternate position (mid-sentence)
slotHints: 主句先说结论，since后补充理由
句中通常不加逗号，避免插入太多成分
templates.exampleSentence: We should leave now since it’s getting late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们现在该走了，因为天越来越晚了。

templates.label: Writing-oriented (objective tone)
slotHints: 背景事实用较客观措辞（e.g., results are…, data show…）
结论用正式动词（require, recommend, will be…）
templates.exampleSentence: Since the data are incomplete, we recommend collecting more samples.
templates.exampleTranslation: 由于数据不完整，我们建议收集更多样本。

templates.label: Advice/permission pattern
slotHints: {condition_clause}=对方已满足的条件/已发生事实
{action}=建议/允许做的事（动词原形）
templates.exampleSentence: Since you’re here, you can join us.
templates.exampleTranslation: 既然你来了，你可以一起参加。

card.coreExplanation: although 引导让步从句，表示“虽然…但是…”。although 后必须接完整句子（主语+谓语），常放句首后加逗号，再接主句；也可放句中，但主句更重要。
card.usageNote: 语气偏正式、书面或中性表达，适合写作、报告、邮件、说明原因与结果的对比；日常口语更常用 though / even though。避免只写 although 从句就结束，必须有主句。句首用“Although…, 主句.” 常配逗号；不要和 but 同时使用。初学者先固定用“Although + 完整句子, 主句”最安全。

slotSchema.label: Although 从句（让步）
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放“完整句子”，包含主语+动词。常错：只放名词/短语。例：Although it was raining...（不是 Although the rain...）

slotSchema.label: 主句（结果/决定/事实）
slotSchema.hint: 放主要信息，通常不再加 but。常错：Although..., but...。例：Although I was tired, I finished the work.

slotSchema.label: 可选强调词
slotSchema.hint: 可在主句用 still / nevertheless 表“仍然”。常错：重复让步或位置乱。例：Although it’s expensive, I still want it.

commonErrors: Although it was raining. → 只有让步从句，句子不完整；加主句：Although it was raining, we went out.
Although it was raining, but we went out. → although 已表达“虽然”，再用 but 重复；删 but：Although it was raining, we went out.
Although the bad weather, we canceled the trip. → although 后不能接名词短语；改为从句：Although the weather was bad, we canceled the trip. 或用 despite：Despite the bad weather, ...
Although to study hard, he failed. → although 后不能直接接不定式；改为完整句：Although he studied hard, he failed.
I went out although it was raining, I was late. → 逗号连接两个主句是逗号拼接；改为一个主句或用连词：I went out although it was raining, and I was late.
Although he is young, he is but experienced. → but 位置与用法错误且重复；改为：Although he is young, he is experienced. 或：He is young but experienced.

contrasts.title: even though + clause
contrasts.note: 结构同样是 + 从句；语气更强，更强调“出乎意料仍然…”，口语更常见。对比：Although it was late, I called.（中性/书面） vs Even though it was late, I called.（更强调）

contrasts.title: though + clause
contrasts.note: 结构相近，可句首/句中/句末；though 更口语，句末作补充更自然。对比：Although I like it, I won’t buy it.（较正式） vs I like it, though I won’t buy it.（口语/补充）

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: despite 后接名词或动名词，不接完整句子；更紧凑、书面。对比：Although it was raining, we played.（从句） vs Despite the rain, we played.（名词短语）

contrasts.title: in spite of + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 意义≈despite，但更长、更正式；同样后接名词/动名词。对比：Despite the cost, I bought it.（更简洁） vs In spite of the cost, I bought it.（更正式/更长）

contrasts.title: even if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构是 + 从句，但表示“即使（假设）…也…”，强调假设而非事实让步。对比：Although it is raining, we’ll go.（事实：在下雨） vs Even if it rains, we’ll go.（假设：就算下雨也去）

examples.sentence: Although I’m tired, I’ll finish it.
examples.translation: 虽然我累了，但我会把它做完。
examples.note: 最安全的基础结构：句首 although + 逗号 + 主句。

examples.sentence: I’ll finish it although I’m tired.
examples.translation: 我会把它做完，虽然我很累。
examples.note: although 从句放句末，语气更像补充说明。

examples.sentence: Although the results were not perfect, the study provides useful evidence.
examples.translation: 虽然结果并不完美，这项研究仍提供了有用的证据。
examples.note: 偏写作/学术表达，主句信息更重要。

examples.sentence: Although he studied hard, he didn’t pass the exam.
examples.translation: 虽然他很努力学习，但他还是没通过考试。
examples.note: 对比敏感：不要写 Although..., but...

examples.sentence: Although it’s expensive, I still want to buy it.
examples.translation: 虽然很贵，我还是想买。
examples.note: 主句用 still 强化“仍然”。

examples.sentence: Although I don’t agree with you, I understand your point.
examples.translation: 虽然我不同意你，但我理解你的观点。
examples.note: 适合礼貌表达不同意见。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: {concession_clause}=完整句子（主语+动词），如 I’m busy / it was raining
{main_clause}=主要结论或行动，不要再加 but
templates.exampleSentence: Although I’m busy, I can help you.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然我很忙，但我可以帮你。

templates.label: Alternate-position template (although at end)
slotHints: 先说主要信息，再用 although 补充让步原因
句末通常不加逗号，但注意整句只保留一个主句
templates.exampleSentence: I went to the meeting although I was sick.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然我生病了，我还是去开会了。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
slotHints: 适合书面扩展，用分号连接第二个结果句
{extra_result_clause}=另一个完整句子，如 therefore... / as a result...
templates.exampleSentence: Although the sample size was small, the findings are consistent; further research is needed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然样本量很小，但研究结果一致；仍需要进一步研究。

templates.label: Polite disagreement template
slotHints: {concession_clause}=对对方的肯定/理解，如 I see your point
{main_clause}=自己的立场，用 understand/agree/think 等动词更礼貌
templates.exampleSentence: Although I understand your concern, I think we should try it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然我理解你的担心，但我觉得我们应该试一试。

card.coreExplanation: 用 if 引出“真实/可能发生”的条件：If + 一般现在时从句，主句用 will/can + 动词原形（或祈使句/一般现在）。if 从句常放句首后加逗号，也可放句末不加逗号。
card.usageNote: 语气多为中性，常用于计划、建议、规则说明、日常安排（先说条件，再说结果/允许/能力）。不适合表达“几乎不可能/纯想象”的情况，那更像 if + 过去式, would。注意：if 从句不能用 will（除非表示意愿/坚持），句首 if 从句后通常加逗号；把 if 从句放句末更口语。初学者优先用“if + 现在时，will + 原形”最安全。

slotSchema.label: If-clause (condition)
slotSchema.hint: 放一般现在时表达条件（I/you/he + V/does）。常错：用 will：If it will rain... 改 If it rains... 例：If you study, ...

slotSchema.label: Main clause (result)
slotSchema.hint: 结果常用 will/can + 动词原形，或祈使句。常错：主句用现在进行/缺少动词原形：will going。改 will go。例：..., you can join us.

slotSchema.label: Position & comma
slotSchema.hint: if 从句在句首要加逗号；在句末通常不加。常错：句首不加逗号或乱加。例：If you’re free, call me / Call me if you’re free.

commonErrors: If it will rain, we will stay home.｜if 从句通常不用 will 表将来；用一般现在时。改：If it rains, we’ll stay home.
If you will finish your homework, you can play.｜这里不是“意愿/坚持”，是条件；if 从句用一般现在。改：If you finish your homework, you can play.
If he go to the party, I will go too.｜第三人称单数现在时要加 -s。改：If he goes to the party, I’ll go too.
If you are free you can come.｜句首 if 从句后少逗号，读起来不清晰。改：If you are free, you can come.
If you will come, call me.｜想表达“如果你来”，不用 will；用 present。改：If you come, call me.
If you study, you will can pass.｜will 与 can 不能叠加；二选一或改写。改：If you study, you can pass / you will pass.

contrasts.title: unless + clause
contrasts.note: 结构：unless = if ... not。语气更像“除非/否则”，常用于规则或警告。对比：If you don’t hurry, you’ll miss the bus. = Unless you hurry, you’ll miss the bus.

contrasts.title: if + past, would + base
contrasts.note: 结构差异：if 从句用过去式，主句 would，表示不太可能/假设。语气更虚拟、更委婉。对比：If it rains, we’ll stay home（可能下雨） vs If it rained, we’d stay home（假设）。

contrasts.title: as long as + clause
contrasts.note: 结构：as long as 强调“只要…就…”，条件更像最低要求/保证。语气更强调限制。对比：If you pay, you can enter（条件） vs As long as you pay, you can enter（只要付钱就行）。

contrasts.title: when + present, (future result)
contrasts.note: 结构：when 表时间必然发生，if 表条件不一定发生。语气：when 更确定。对比：When you arrive, call me（你一定会到） vs If you arrive, call me（如果你到了的话）。

contrasts.title: Do..., and/or ...
contrasts.note: 结构：祈使句 + and/or 表结果；更口语、更直接。对比：If you press this button, the door will open. vs Press this button, and the door will open.

examples.sentence: If it rains, we’ll stay home.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就待在家。
examples.note: 核心：if 现在时 + will 原形。

examples.sentence: We’ll stay home if it rains.
examples.translation: 如果下雨，我们就待在家。
examples.note: 从句后置更口语，通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: If you finish your work, you can leave early.
examples.translation: 如果你完成工作，你可以早点走。
examples.note: 主句用 can 表“允许/可以”。

examples.sentence: If you don’t feel well, see a doctor.
examples.translation: 如果你不舒服，就去看医生。
examples.note: 主句可用祈使句给建议。

examples.sentence: If you need any further information, please contact us.
examples.translation: 如需更多信息，请联系我们。
examples.note: 较书面/邮件用法：礼貌且正式一点。

examples.sentence: If I have time tomorrow, I’ll call you. (Not: If I will have time...)
examples.translation: 如果我明天有时间，我会给你打电话。（不要说：If I will have time...）
examples.note: 对比敏感点：if 从句不用 will 表将来。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
templates.template: If + 一般现在时（条件）, I will + 动词原形（结果）。
slotHints: 条件用一般现在时：I/you/we/they + V；he/she/it + V-s
主句用 will + 原形，不要 will can 叠加
句首 if 从句后加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: If I finish early, I will go home.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果我提前完成，我就回家。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
templates.template: 主句（will/can/祈使句） + if + 一般现在时（条件）。
slotHints: 后置 if 从句更口语
后置时通常不加逗号
条件仍用一般现在时
templates.exampleSentence: Call me if you need help.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你需要帮助就给我打电话。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: If + 一般现在时（条件）, please + 动词原形（请求/指引）。
slotHints: 适合邮件/通知：please 更礼貌
if 从句句首加逗号
动词用原形：please contact/let us know
templates.exampleSentence: If you have any questions, please let me know.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你有任何问题，请告诉我。

card.coreExplanation: 用“动词 + that + 从句”把你认为/说/知道的内容当作一句完整信息来报告。that后面必须是带主语和谓语的完整句子。常放在think/say/know等动词后作宾语。
card.usageNote: 语气中性到偏正式，适合写作、讨论、转述信息：I think that… / She said that… 口语里that常可省略，但初学写作建议先保留更清楚。不适合表达“是否”的问题（要用whether/if），也不用于直接疑问句语序。that从句紧跟动词，不用逗号隔开；否定多放主句动词前（I don’t think that…）更自然。

slotSchema.label: Reporting verb
slotSchema.hint: 放think/say/know/hope/believe等“陈述/认知”动词。常错：用ask后接that（ask通常接宾语+to或wh/if/whether）。例：I think… / She said…

slotSchema.label: that + full clause
slotSchema.hint: that后接“主语+谓语”的完整句。常错：只放名词或短语。例：that he is right（对）；✗that his idea（少谓语）

slotSchema.label: Tense & pronoun choice
slotSchema.hint: 从句时态按“实际时间”或“转述时态”选择；代词按说话人切换。常错：代词不改。例：She said that she was tired（不是I）

commonErrors: ✗I think that is a good idea.｜把that误当作从句引导词但后面缺从句主语/谓语；这里that是代词“那”。｜改：I think that it is a good idea. 或 I think it is a good idea.
✗He said that did not come.｜that后缺主语，导致从句不完整。｜改：He said that he did not come.
✗I don’t think that does he like it.｜把从句写成疑问句倒装；that从句用陈述语序。｜改：I don’t think that he likes it.
✗I asked that he was OK.｜ask一般不接that从句来表示“问”；应使用if/whether或wh从句。｜改：I asked if/whether he was OK.
✗I think, that we should go.｜动词和that从句之间不加逗号。｜改：I think that we should go.
✗I know that whether he will come.｜that与whether重复引导；二选一。｜改：I know whether he will come. 或 I know that he will come.

contrasts.title: verb + whether/if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构：whether/if引导“是否”问题；that引导“陈述内容”。语气：whether更正式。对比：I know that he is coming（我知道他会来） vs I know whether/if he is coming（我知道他会不会来/是否会来）

contrasts.title: verb + wh- clause (embedded question)
contrasts.note: 结构：wh从句表达“谁/什么/哪里/为什么”等信息缺口；that从句是完整陈述。语气：wh更像“间接问句”。对比：I know that he left（我知道他离开了） vs I know why he left（我知道他为什么离开）

contrasts.title: omit that (spoken)
contrasts.note: 结构：口语可省that：think/say + 从句；写作保留that更清晰。场景：聊天省略更自然，论文/报告保留更稳。对比：I think (that) it’s true（都对，但写作更常用that）

contrasts.title: noun + that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构：名词后that从句多作同位语解释内容；这里是动词后的宾语从句。语气：名词结构更书面。对比：I believe that it’s important（动词） vs the belief that it’s important（名词）

contrasts.title: direct quote vs that-clause report
contrasts.note: 结构：直接引语用引号/冒号；that从句是间接转述。语气：直接引语更生动，that从句更客观。对比：She said, “I’m busy.” vs She said that she was busy.

examples.sentence: I think that this plan will work.
examples.translation: 我认为这个计划会奏效。
examples.note: 最基础：think + that + 完整句

examples.sentence: She said that she was tired, so she went home early.
examples.translation: 她说她累了，所以她早早回家了。
examples.note: 转述时注意代词与时态

examples.sentence: That he apologized surprised me.
examples.translation: 他道歉这件事让我很惊讶。
examples.note: 同样是that从句，但放句首作主语，较书面

examples.sentence: I don’t think that he knows the answer.
examples.translation: 我不认为他知道答案。
examples.note: 否定通常放在think前更自然

examples.sentence: I don’t know whether he will come, but I know that he wants to come.
examples.translation: 我不知道他会不会来，但我知道他想来。
examples.note: 对比whether/if（是否）与that（陈述）

examples.sentence: My teacher says (that) we should review every day.
examples.translation: 我老师说我们应该每天复习。
examples.note: 口语可省that，但保留更清楚

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: S用人/事物作主语（he/this plan/my friend）
V用一般现在/将来等陈述语序，不倒装
不确定时先保留that更安全
templates.exampleSentence: I think that she needs more time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我认为她需要更多时间。

templates.label: Alternate (negative) template
templates.template: I don’t think that + S + V + (O).
slotHints: 把否定放主句don’t think更自然
that从句仍用肯定/否定陈述语序
别写成疑问倒装：✗does he…
templates.exampleSentence: I don’t think that this is a good idea.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我不认为这是个好主意。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: It is clear that + S + V + (O).
slotHints: 更偏正式写作/报告
that后必须是完整句
句中不加逗号分隔it is clear和that从句
templates.exampleSentence: It is clear that the results support our claim.
templates.exampleTranslation: 很明显，这些结果支持我们的主张。

templates.label: Reporting template
slotHints: X可用人名/代词（my boss/he）
注意代词转换：I→he/she
转述过去常用said that + 过去时（按语境）
templates.exampleSentence: My friend said that he couldn’t join us.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我朋友说他不能加入我们。

card.coreExplanation: 用“名词 + who + 从句”来限定或补充说明“人”。who后必须接完整从句，who在从句里充当主语（后面常接动词）。通常紧跟在所修饰的名词后，放句中名词位置。
card.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都常用；适合介绍人物、讲故事、描述同学同事等。避免用来指物或地点（改用which/that/where），也别在名词前面单独放who从句。who从句一般不用逗号（限定性）；如果是补充说明、可删掉也不影响指代，需用逗号并更偏书面。初学最稳：先写“the person/student/man + who + 动词…”。

slotSchema.label: Person noun phrase
slotSchema.hint: 放“人”的名词短语：the man/my friend/a student。常错：用物作先行词。例：the teacher…（不是 the book…）

slotSchema.label: who + clause (who as subject)
slotSchema.hint: who后接“动词 + (宾语/补语…)”。常错：缺动词或把宾语位置也用who。例：who lives in Shanghai / who works here（不是 who in Shanghai）

slotSchema.label: Main clause (rest of sentence)
slotSchema.hint: 整个名词短语在主句里当主语/宾语等。常错：重复代词。例：The man who called me is here.（不是 The man who called me he is here.）

commonErrors: The person who she is my teacher...：who从句里又加she，主语重复；改：The person who is my teacher... 或 The person that/whom I like...（按角色选）
The book who is on the table...：who只能指人；改：The book which/that is on the table...
The man who I met yesterday...：这里who在从句里是宾语，不是主语；改：The man (whom/that) I met yesterday... 或口语可省略宾语关系词：The man I met yesterday...
The girl, who studies hard is my sister.：限定性从句不该随便加逗号；改：The girl who studies hard is my sister.（若只是补充说明才用逗号）
The student who studying hard will pass.：who后从句谓语形式错误；改：The student who studies hard will pass. 或 who is studying hard...
The man who works in the company that is tall...：从句堆叠导致指代不清；改：The tall man who works in the company... 或分句：The man works in the company. He is tall.

contrasts.title: who vs that (people)
contrasts.note: 结构：who更专指人；that可指人/物，结构同“名词 + that + 从句”。语气：that更口语、更泛用；who更自然、更清晰指人。对比：the man who called me（更标准）/ the man that called me（口语也可）

contrasts.title: who vs which (things)
contrasts.note: 结构：who只修饰人；which修饰物/事。语气：which偏中性到书面，不能用于人。对比：the teacher who teaches math（人）/ the course which is difficult（物/事）

contrasts.title: who (subject) vs whom (object)
contrasts.note: 结构：who在从句里做主语；whom做宾语，常见“名词 + whom + 主语 + 动词”。语气：whom更正式，口语常用that或省略。对比：the man who helped me（主语）/ the man whom I helped（宾语，更正式）

contrasts.title: who vs whose (possession)
contrasts.note: 结构：whose后必须接名词，表示所属：noun + whose + noun + 从句。语气：写作常用，信息更具体。对比：the girl who has a dog（她有狗）/ the girl whose dog is cute（她的狗很可爱）

contrasts.title: No comma vs comma (defining vs extra info)
contrasts.note: 结构：限定性who从句通常不用逗号；非限定性用逗号包起来。语气：带逗号更像补充说明、更书面。对比：My brother who lives in Beijing is a doctor（我有不止一个兄弟）/ My brother, who lives in Beijing, is a doctor（只有一个兄弟，补充信息）

examples.sentence: I have a friend who speaks Chinese very well.
examples.translation: 我有一个朋友中文说得很好。
examples.note: 最基础：先行词是人，who在从句里做主语。

examples.sentence: The teacher who teaches us English is strict.
examples.translation: 教我们英语的老师很严格。
examples.note: 名词短语作主语；who后接动词teaches。

examples.sentence: I met a woman who works at a bank.
examples.translation: 我遇到了一位在银行工作的女士。
examples.note: 名词短语作宾语；who从句紧跟woman。

examples.sentence: My brother, who lives in Beijing, is visiting next week.
examples.translation: 我哥哥住在北京，他下周要来拜访。
examples.note: 非限定性：补充说明，用逗号，更偏书面/正式口吻。

examples.sentence: The man who helped me yesterday is here; the man whom I helped is not.
examples.translation: 昨天帮助我的那个人在这儿；我帮助过的那个人不在。
examples.note: 对比敏感：who=主语；whom=宾语（更正式）。

examples.sentence: Do you know the student who sits next to Lily?
examples.translation: 你认识坐在莉莉旁边的那个学生吗？
examples.note: 疑问句里也一样：先行词后立刻接who从句。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: [person]=friend/classmate/teacher 等“人”的名词短语
[verb]=lives/works/studies/speaks 等第三人称单数动词
[rest]=地点/宾语/状语，如 in Shanghai / English well
templates.exampleSentence: I have a classmate who lives near my school.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我有一个同学住在我学校附近。

templates.label: Alternate-position (person NP as object)
slotHints: 动词可用 met/saw/know/liked 等
先行词后立刻接who从句，不要隔开
who从句里不要再加 he/she
templates.exampleSentence: I saw a man who carries a big bag every day.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我看到一个男人每天都提着一个大包。

templates.label: Writing-oriented (nondefining)
slotHints: 逗号必须成对出现，表示“补充信息”
适合已明确的人名/唯一身份，如 Tom / my father
口语少用，写作更自然
templates.exampleSentence: Tom, who studies history, wants to be a teacher.
templates.exampleTranslation: 汤姆学历史，他想当老师。
